mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or out-of-sample hit rate falls below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate broad-based consumer demand expansion that directly boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with a Rank IC > 0.04 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which reduces input cost pressures and generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO changes yield negative or flat 5-day forward returns in > 55% of observations during late-cycle phases, or regime-specific IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns remain positive in > 60% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 250.0, or the signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.02) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirm robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer demand, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint PAYEMS-UNRATE signal fails to produce a statistically significant positive return differential (p > 0.05) over 60-day horizons in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth rates above trend indicate sustained economic expansion that lifts corporate profit margins and equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth signals yield negative 20-day forward returns in > 50% of quarters, or the quarterly data frequency causes persistent look-ahead bias that invalidates the monthly prediction.

mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates combined with elevated consumer price indices create a stagflationary policy environment that suppresses equity risk premiums and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns during tightening cycles.
failure_condition: The interaction term between FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL shows no incremental predictive power over single-series models, or 20-day returns are positive in > 65% of tightening regime observations.
